title: Fermion condensation in a generalized Hatsugai-Kohmoto model with momentum-mixing Landau interactions
authors: Jan Heinrich, Andreas Rückriegel, Peter Kopietz
abstract: The Hatsugai-Kohmoto (HK) model is an exactly solvable electronic lattice model where the interaction between electrons with opposite spin is diagonal in momentum space. We generalize the HK model by introducing momentum-mixing Landau interactions. Within a self-consistent mean-field analysis we find that the ground state of this model exhibits a partially flat energy band, in agreement with the fermion condensation scenario proposed by Khodel and Shaginyan [JETP Lett. 51, 553 (1990)]. Inspired by Andersons pseudospin formulation of BCS theory, we show that the HK model with Landau interactions can be mapped onto a generalized Ising model where each site of the reciprocal lattice hosts two Ising spins. In the pseudospin picture the emergence of a partially flat electronic band corresponds to the smoothing of a magnetic domain wall. Moreover, guided by the pseudospin picture, we propose an exactly solvable variant of the HK model which has a unique ground state for all densities.
summary: This paper explores how momentum-dependent interactions modify the electronic structure of the Hatsugai-Kohmoto model. It demonstrates that these interactions can induce a partially flat energy band, providing a theoretical mechanism for fermion condensation. The study also provides a mapping between the electronic model and a generalized Ising model.
main_problem: Investigating whether momentum-mixing Landau interactions in a generalized Hatsugai-Kohmoto model lead to the emergence of a partially flat energy band (fermion condensation).
main_result: The study finds that momentum-mixing interactions smooth out magnetic domain walls in a pseudospin representation, resulting in a partially flat electronic band consistent with the fermion condensation scenario.
method: The authors employ a self-consistent mean-field analysis and a pseudospin formulation that maps the model onto a generalized Ising model in reciprocal space.
model_or_system: A generalized Hatsugai-Kohmoto (HKL) model describing electrons on a d-dimensional hypercubic lattice with kinetic energy, Hubbard-like interaction (U), and momentum-mixing Landau-type interactions.
key_observables: Momentum distribution (n_k), Fermi surfaces, energy dispersion, pseudomagnetization (m_k), and the excitation gap (Delta).
important_parameters: Interaction strength (U), bandwidth (W), particle density (rho), and momentum-mixing interaction parameters (f_kk').
main_assumptions: The HKL model is not exactly solvable, necessitating a mean-field approximation; the interaction range is assumed to be finite in reciprocal space.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows ground state configurations and momentum distributions for different phases (HSD, HS, SD, S); Figure 6 displays the local magnetic moment, effective magnetic field, and the phase diagram in the U vs. h_k plane.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the generalized HK model, develops a pseudospin mapping to an Ising model, performs a mean-field analysis to identify the fermion condensation phase, and concludes by proposing an exactly solvable variant of the model.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Left figure: ground state occupation of a one- dimensional energy band in the phase HSD where all three types of occupations coexist in the band. Here and in all subsequent figures empty states (holes) are shaded red, singly occupied states are shaded green, and doubly occupied states are shaded blue. The horizontal dashed lines mark the Fermi energies ϵD and ϵS where the occupation changes discontin- uously. Right figure: momentum distribution in the HSD phase. The vertical dashed lines mark the positions of the Fermi momenta kD and kS. All wavevectors are measured in units of the inverse lattice spacing. (b)-(d) are analogous illustrations of the phases HS, SD, and S.

FIG. 2. Phase diagram of the HK model as a function of the density ρ and the interaction U. The color coding indicates the occupancies of momentum states: holes (H, red), singly occupied states (S, green), and doubly occupied states (D, blue). The insets represent the corresponding band fillings.

FIG. 4. Phase diagram of the HK model as a function of the chemical potential µ/U and band width W/U in units of the interaction U. The thick black lines represent the chemical potentials µ+, µ−defined in Eq. (38). The thick red lines represents µS = −W/2, while the thick blue line represents µD = U + W/2.
FIG. 3. Critical interaction Uc(ρ) separating the HSD phase from the HS phase and the SD phase for a d-dimensional tight-binding dispersion of the type (37) in d = 1, 2, 3.
FIG. 5. (a) Local magnetic moment mk = ⟨Sz k⟩of the pseu- dospin HK model (48) as function of the pseudomagnetic field hk. (b) Phase diagram of the HK model in the plane spanned by hk and U.

Spin-momentum entanglement.note: The paper directly studies how momentum-mixing Landau interactions modify the electronic structure and spin-dependent energy dispersion, which is a central mechanism for creating spin-momentum correlations.
